deck: Glasswing International co-founder and president Celina de Sola on building a community-driven, non-clinical model for mental health care across Latin America and New York City, and using technology and data to scale impact.
seo_description: Glasswing International's Celina de Sola on a community-driven, non-clinical mental health model and using technology and data to scale impact across Latin America.
og_description: Glasswing's Celina de Sola on community-driven mental health care and scaling impact with technology and data.
about: Glasswing International is an NGO that has impacted more than 2.2 million lives through education and health initiatives across Latin America and New York City. Co-founder and president Celina de Sola shares how the organization evolved and grew to 600 employees, and how its community-driven approach shaped a non-clinical model for mental health care.
The conversation explores scalability, sustainability, and the role of technology and data in surfacing both growth and the gaps, so organizations can reach greater impact.
bullets: A remarkable journey: how Glasswing International grew to impact more than 2.2 million lives across Latin America and New York City
Scaling the team: building an organization of 600 employees while staying community driven
A non-clinical model for mental health care, shaped by a community-driven approach
Technology and data as tools for highlighting both growth and the gaps that remain
Scalability and sustainability: what it takes for an NGO to reach greater impact over the long run
